findings.summary: Component count of 37 and type breakdown are accurate; AP3012 boost converter output_rail reported as 3.3V instead of 5V; AP3012 assumed_vref=0.6V is wrong; actual Vref is 1.23V, so estimated_vout=...

correct.description: The schematic correctly identifies 37 total components including 8 resistors, 4 jumpers, 2 capacitors, 2 transistors (BSS138), 1 IC (AP3012), 1 inductor, 1 diode, 1 LED, 3 connectors, 4 fiducials, 4 mounting holes, 2 test points, and 4 aesthetic logo parts. Power rails 3.3V, 5V, GND, and VCC are all correctly detected.

The analyzer correctly identifies U1 (AP3012) as a switching regulator with topology='switching', inductor='L1', input_rail='3.3V'. The feedback network R1 (100k) / R2 (33k) is correctly identified as a feedback network with is_feedback=true and correctly connects to the 5V output in the voltage_divider analysis (top_net=5V). Decoupling capacitors C1 (3.3V rail) and C2 (5V rail) are both detected.

Q1 and Q2 (BSS138 N-MOSFETs) implement the standard bidirectional I2C level shifter. Both are correctly identified as type='mosfet', is_pchannel=false, with gate_net=3.3V, source on the 3.3V-side I2C signals (SDA/SCL), and drain on the 5V-side signals (HV_SDA/HV_SCL). The circuit provides voltage translation between 3.3V Qwiic I2C and the selectable high-voltage I2C bus.

The test_coverage section correctly identifies 2 test points: TP1 on the SDA net and TP2 on the SCL net, covering the low-voltage I2C bus. The uncovered key nets (HV_SDA, HV_SCL, VCC) are also correctly identified.

incorrect.description: The AP3012 (U1) is a step-up (boost) converter. Its IN pin (pin 5) is on the 3.3V net (correct as input), its SW pin drives through Schottky diode D2 (PMEG4005EJ), and D2's cathode is on the 5V net — the true output rail. The analyzer correctly traces the inductor L1 but misassigns output_rail to the inductor's input net (3.3V) rather than the Schottky diode cathode net (5V). The voltage_divider feedback network (R1=100k top, R2=33k bottom) also correctly shows top_net=5V, confirming the output is 5V.

The AP3012 datasheet specifies a feedback reference voltage of 1.23V (not 0.6V as assumed by the analyzer). With R1=100k and R2=33k, the correct Vout = 1.23V * (1 + 100k/33k) ≈ 4.96V, consistent with the 5V output rail. The analyzer uses a generic 0.6V heuristic, producing an incorrect estimated_vout of 2.418V and a misleading 26.7% mismatch warning against the 3.3V net name. Both the Vref and Vout estimates are wrong.

Q1 and Q2 (BSS138 N-MOSFETs) form a classic bidirectional I2C level shifter circuit with their gates tied directly to the 3.3V power rail. The analyzer incorrectly lists U1 (AP3012) as a gate_driver_ic for both MOSFETs because U1's input pin is also on the 3.3V net. AP3012 does not drive the MOSFET gates; the gates are statically biased from the power rail.

findings.summary: PCB statistics are accurate: 2-layer, 25.4x25.4mm, fully routed; AP3012 boost IC referenced as U5 in PCB but U1 in schematic output — multi-project schematic artifact; GND copper pours on both F.Cu...

correct.description: The PCB correctly reports a 2-layer board (F.Cu, B.Cu), 25.4x25.4mm dimensions, 69 total footprints (28 SMD + 1 THT + 35 board-only + 4 solder mask bridge + 1 exclude-from-pos), 153 track segments, 94 vias, 5 zones, 21 nets, and routing_complete=true. The high via count (94) reflects heavy GND stitching on the small board.

The thermal analysis correctly identifies a GND zone spanning both F.Cu and B.Cu (645.2mm² area) with 77 stitching vias at 11.9/cm² density. Separate 3.3V (41.7mm²) and 5V (33.9mm²) zones on F.Cu are also correctly identified. The zone analysis reflects the compact 25.4×25.4mm board with heavy power plane coverage.

DFM reports dfm_tier='standard', min_track_width=0.254mm, min_drill=0.3mm, min_annular_ring=0.13mm, and zero violations. These metrics are appropriate for a standard Qwiic breakout board manufactured at typical PCB fab tolerances.

incorrect.description: The KiCad schematic file is shared across multiple projects. The schematic analyzer reads the 'co2' project instance references (U1 for AP3012) while the PCB uses references from a different project instance (U5). Both correctly point to the same physical AP3012 IC, but cross-referencing schematic vs PCB analysis outputs is misleading. The PCB component_groups 'U' group lists only U5.

suggestions: Fix: AP3012 boost IC referenced as U5 in PCB but U1 in schematic output — multi-project schematic artifact
